qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cluster websocket proxy bypasses secure-session enforcement on terminal access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets ProxyWs route ordering and secure-session enforcement on proxied PTY access, not the advisory's missing WebSocket origin validation and cookie-authenticated CSWSH root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated site health-check feature permits SSRF to attacker-chosen internal destinations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets attacker-controlled outbound health-check requests and missing destination restrictions, which is unrelated to the advisory's WebSocket origin-validation flaw.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described CSWSH root cause of permissive WebSocket origin handling on authenticated endpoints combined with browser-cookie authentication.
evidence: GHSA-78mf-482w-62qj describes Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking caused by missing origin validation on authenticated WebSocket endpoints and browser cookies being usable for those upgrades.
cluster_terminal_secure_session_bypass instead describes a ProxyWs ordering flaw that skips RequireSecureSession for x_node_id-proxied terminal access.
sitecheck_authenticated_ssrf instead describes attacker-controlled site metadata and health-check settings steering outbound HTTP or gRPC requests to internal destinations.

Inspection of the PoC-pinned upstream snapshot showed WebSocket routes using AuthRequiredWS and CheckWebSocketOrigin rather than the advisory's described any-origin, cookie-authenticated WebSocket behavior.
